### Code Snippet 2 (score: 0.722, file: /aistor/sjtu/hpc_stor01/home/liuxiang/LLM4NPU/LLM4Kernel/ascendCode/mindspore_graphengine/910/third_party/fwkacllib/inc/ops/nn_pooling_ops.h)

```cpp
/**
* @brief Applies a 2D adaptive max pooling over an input signal conposed of several input planes.
* The output is of size H x W, for any input size.

* @par Inputs:
* One input, including:
* @li x: A Tensor. Must be one of the following data types:
*     float16, float32, float64. \n

* @par Attributes:
* @li output_size: A required list of 2 ints
*    specifying the size (H,W) of the output tensor. \n

* @par Outputs:
* @li y: A Tensor. Has the same data type as "x" \n

* @par Third-party framework compatibility
* Compatible with the Pytorch operator AdaptiveMaxPool2d.
*/
REG_OP(AdaptiveMaxPool2d)
    .INPUT(x, TensorType({DT_FLOAT16, DT_FLOAT32, DT_DOUBLE}))
    .OUTPUT(y, TensorType({DT_FLOAT16, DT_FLOAT32, DT_DOUBLE}))
    .OUTPUT(argmax, TensorType::IndexNumberType())
    .REQUIRED_ATTR(output_size, ListInt)
    .OP_END_FACTORY_REG(AdaptiveMaxPool2d)

/**
* @brief Computes second-order gradients of the maxpooling3d function .

* @par Inputs:
* @li orig_x: Original forward input tensor(NDC1HWC0) of type float16
* @li orig_y: Original forward output tensor(NDC1HWC0) of type float16
* @li grads: Gradient tensor(NDC1HWC0) of type float16. \n

* @par Attributes:
* @li ksize: A required list or tuple,
* specifying the size of the sliding window.
* @li strides: A required list or tuple,
* specifying the stride of the sliding window.
* @li pads: A required list or tuple
* @li data_format: An optional string. Format of the original input, either NCDHW or NDHWC. Defaults to NDHWC . \n

* @attention Constraints:
* @li Only the Ascend 910 platform is supported.
* @li "orig_x" and "grads" must have the same shape.
* @li "orig_y" and "y" must have the same shape. Otherwise, an error is reported.
* @li "orig_x", "orig_y", "grads", and "y" must be NDC1HWC0 tensors . \n

* @par Outputs:
* @li y: Result tensor of type float16
```

tool_usage.query:
Ascend C max_pooling_1d kernel implementation
API: MaxPool AvgPool
AscendC custom kernel implementation for max pooling 1D or 2D (MaxPool1d / MaxPool2d). Look for sliding window max using TPipe/TQue and elementwise Max across shifted windows; examples of pooling kernels in ascend op-plugin or custom operator repositories. Also check usage of AscendC::Max, ReduceMax, DataCopy, and tiling strategies for pooling.
tool_usage.response:
### Code Snippet 1 (score: 0.716, file: /aistor/sjtu/hpc_stor01/home/liuxiang/LLM4NPU/LLM4Kernel/ascendCode/ascend_DrivingSDK/kernels/op_kernel/max_pool2d.cpp)

```cpp
extern "C" __global__ __aicore__ void max_pool2d(GM_ADDR x_trans, GM_ADDR y_trans, GM_ADDR workspace, GM_ADDR tiling)
{
    TPipe pipe;
    GET_TILING_DATA(tiling_data, tiling);
    KernelMaxPool2d op;
    op.Init(x_trans, y_trans, &tiling_data, &pipe);
    op.Process();
}
```
